author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Inshore Fishing Tournament
summary: All proceeds benefit the Block Island Volunteer Fire and Rescue
content:
All proceeds benefit the Block Island Volunteer Fire and Rescue

REGISTRATION AVAILABLE ONLINE AND AT BLOCK ISLAND FISHWORKS ON OCEAN AV

 

When:  5:33 a.m., Saturday, July 24 to 12:00 p.m., Sunday, July 25.  ‘After Party’ and brief awards ceremony to take place 2:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m. at Capt. Nick’s Rock & Roll Bar, Block Island.

Where:  fish within the three-mile limit around Block Island, ‘After Party’ at Capt. Nick’s Rock & Roll Bar 34 Ocean Avenue, Block Island, Rhode Island.

What:  the Block Island Inshore Fishing Tournament is back after a short hiatus.  The Tournament host is Capt. Chris Willi of Block Island Fishworks.  Tournament highlights include:

  • cash cards/swag/and sponsor prizes totally $10,000
  • fluke, black sea bass, striped bass, and bluefish with boat, shore, youth, team, and photo contest
  • $500 1st place prize for fluke, black sea bass, striped bass, and bluefish and $200 2nd place prize in boat division; $500 1st place prize for shore division striped bass and bluefish; $500 first-place team prize; plus $200 youth division and photo contest prizes
  • $100 entry fee/vessel for four anglers, $25/person for each additional person; $25/person for shore entries
  • download app to your smartphone, take a photo of fish with a Tournament measuring device and submit entry electronically, no weigh-in this is a fish length tournament only
  • Pick up participant/captain’s bags the day before the event (Friday, July 23) at one of the host or co-sponsor locations
  • Cosponsors include: Block Island Fishworks, Block Island; Ocean State Tackle, Providence; Saltwater Edge, Middletown; Snug Harbor Marina, South Kingstown; Watch Hill Outfitters, Westerly; and the Block Island Wind Farm (Ørsted).
address: Block Island New Shoreham Washington County

author: Robert Lucier
title: Race Week 2021 – Storm Trysail 29th Year
summary: Block Island hosts some colorful events, but there may be nothing quite as spectacular as racing sailboats cutting through the waters surrounding the island during the annual Block Island Race Week.
content:
Block Island hosts some colorful events, but there may be nothing quite as spectacular as racing sailboats cutting through the waters surrounding the island during the annual Block Island Race Week.

Brought to you by Duck Island Yacht Club,  Block Island Yacht Club and Block Island Community Sailing

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

author: Robert Lucier
title: Conserfest (Virtual) 2021
summary: This year the event will take place virtually, with details to come.
content:
This year the event will take place virtually, with details to come.

Originating on Block Island, RI, ConserFest is a 501(c)3 organization focused on raising funds and awareness for environmental conservation and stewardship.  

We are a strictly grassroots effort relying heavily on volunteers for our events and year-round staff, making us a driving force in raising awareness about local environmental issues.

The proceeds and donations from all ConserFest events help to support and fund local efforts dedicated to protecting Block Island's natural environment and raising awareness for the conservation and preservation of local land, heritage, and culture.  

Like us on Facebook at ConserFest and follow us on Instagram @conserfestbi.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Father's Day
summary: The nation’s first Father’s Day was celebrated on June 19, 1910, in the state of Washington.
content:
The nation’s first Father’s Day was celebrated on June 19, 1910, in the state of Washington.

However, it was not until 1972–58 years after President Woodrow Wilson made Mother’s Day official–that the day honoring fathers became a nationwide holiday in the United States.

Give your Dad a hug if you can this Father's Day.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Memorial Day
summary: Memorial Day is a federal holiday honoring the men and women who died while serving in the U.S. military. Originally known as Decoration Day, it originated in the years following the Civil War and became an official federal holiday in 1971.
content:
Memorial Day is a federal holiday honoring the men and women who died while serving in the U.S. military. Originally known as Decoration Day, it originated in the years following the Civil War and became an official federal holiday in 1971.

Many Americans observe Memorial Day by visiting cemeteries or memorials, holding family gatherings, and participating in parades. Unofficially, it marks the beginning of the summer season.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Annual Thanksgiving Walk at Crescent Beach
summary: Many of us, when we think of Rob Lewis, Block Island's conservation visionary, remember him striding down Crescent Beach where he walked almost every day, often barefoot, in all kinds of weather.  Please help us celebrate this great man, who inspired so many people to donate land and resources to support conservation on this beautiful island.  Leashes are required for canine participants. Of course, shoes are optional!
content:
Many of us, when we think of Rob Lewis, Block Island's conservation visionary, remember him striding down Crescent Beach where he walked almost every day, often barefoot, in all kinds of weather.  Please help us celebrate this great man, who inspired so many people to donate land and resources to support conservation on this beautiful island.  Leashes are required for canine participants. Of course, shoes are optional!

Friday November 27 on Crescent Beach

Walk when it’s best for you and your family

Please follow all social distancing guidelines
address: Crescent Beach New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Medical Center Silent Auction
summary: Don't Miss Out!
content:
Don't Miss Out!
We can't have our Annual Fundraiser Dinner this summer, but don't despair, we still can have fun while helping the Block Island Medical Center continue its important mission to deliver the top-flight medical care we all depend on --especially now! 

 
Instead of an in-person event, we're having the first ever BIMC2020 Virtual Fundraiser and Online Silent Auction!
 
There will be many, many fabulous items to bid on from Home Destinations (in NYC, Boston, Florida, and more), to Outdoor Activities (Sailing, Fishing, Kayaking, and other outdoor excursions), to Take-Out Dining (from BI’s many wonderful restaurants), to BI Essentials (a Weber grill, multiple gallons of gasoline for your car, Heating Oil, NE Airline tickets, and so on), to Wine and Spirits, and Original Art Work.
 
Bidding starts Sunday morning, July 26th and ends at midnight on Saturday, August 1st
 
If you want to join our growing list of Event Sponsors or donate items for our fabulous Silent Auction it’s not too late, just let one of us know!
 
There are other ways to help as well, so stay tuned for more information! For now, just Mark Your Calendars. We will be sending you a link soon so that you may Register, begin to review the items, and make a donation if you wish.
 
The Block Island Medical Center needs your support!

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Walking Festival
summary: A lot of people come to Block Island to lie out on the beach or take in the view from a lounge chair on their back deck. But if getting on your feet isn’t too much to ask, there’s now a new something that makes doing so more rewarding.
content:
A lot of people come to Block Island to lie out on the beach or take in the view from a lounge chair on their back deck. But if getting on your feet isn’t too much to ask, there’s now a new something that makes doing so more rewarding.

Block Island's third annual  Walking Festival.

By signing up and paying a modest registration fee(40$), you can join in on three guided walks per day over the two day period – discovering the over 47% of the island that’s reserved for nature. There will also be a welcome reception where all registered participants can meet up, pick up materials and hear about the history and topography of the island.

 

Registration is Block Island Recreation Department http://new-shoreham.com/

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Block Island Walking Festival
meta.description: Block Island is introducing its first-ever Walking Festival that will give participants the opportunity to explore and experience the natural beauty of the island

address: Block Island New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Restaurant Week
summary: Why not add adventures in dining to your to-do list, as this week marks the fifth annual Block Island Restaurant Week from June 10 through 16.
content:
Why not add adventures in dining to your to-do list, as this week marks the fifth annual Block Island Restaurant Week from June 10 through 16.

Just about 20 of the island’s restaurants, from pubs to posh, are offering special dishes at exceptional prices, including breakfast specials and three-course prix-fixe lunches designed to thrill your palate and your pocketbook. With prices ranging from $10 to $14 for lunch and  $21 to $29 for dinner, you'll want to eat out every day and every night you're here. 

Visit the website for a list of participating restaurants, menus, specials and phone numbers. And whichever restaurants you choose, make sure you call ahead for reservations. You don't want to miss a morsel of the most delectable culinary event of the year.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Block Island Restaurant Week
meta.description: Try all sorts of great food during restaurant week on Block Island June 10 through June 16, 2018.

address: New Shoreham New Shoreham Washington County

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Virtual 45th Annual Run Around the Block
summary: This may be the longest run around a block on record. That’s because the particular block in question is actually Block Island, a little piece of paradise just 7 miles long and 3 miles wide off the coast of Rhode Island. And you’ll see most of its beauty — rolling meadows, stone walls, sparkling ponds, towering cliffs —during the Run Around the Block, a 9.3 mile (15K) journey around this stunning island where more than 45% of the area is conservation land.
content:
This may be the longest run around a block on record. That’s because the particular block in question is actually Block Island, a little piece of paradise just 7 miles long and 3 miles wide off the coast of Rhode Island. And you’ll see most of its beauty — rolling meadows, stone walls, sparkling ponds, towering cliffs —during the Run Around the Block, a 9.3 mile (15K) journey around this stunning island where more than 45% of the area is conservation land.

The race starts at 11:45 am on Saturday, September 12. For locals, September is their favorite time of year. The days are still long, the water is still warm, and the summer crowds have thinned out since Labor Day. Most island businesses will still be open, so you’ll find plenty of places to eat, drink, relax and shop before you make the trip back to the mainland.

Run Around the Block has a fairly late starting time, so runners coming over that morning have plenty of time to catch a morning ferry and make it to the race on time. Free bus transportation will take you from ferry to race and back again.

Registration costs $30, and pre-registration is available at www.active.com.

meta.title: 42nd Annual Run Around the Block
meta.description: The 42nd Annual Run Around the Block is on September 8, 2018.

address: Center Rd New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Virtual Mary D. Ball
summary: From beaching to boating, hiking to biking, there are tons of ways to have a ball on Block Island. But there’s one Ball (note the capital B) that outshines them all for its festive family-friendly atmosphere and its importance to local islanders. It’s the annual Mary D. Ball, the premier fundraiser for the Mary D. Fund, started by Block Island’s charitable force of nature Mary Donnelly, Block Island nurse for more than 50 years until she retired in 2013 at age 86.
content:
From beaching to boating, hiking to biking, there are tons of ways to have a ball on Block Island. But there’s one Ball (note the capital B) that outshines them all for its festive family-friendly atmosphere and its importance to local islanders. It’s the annual Mary D. Ball, the premier fundraiser for the Mary D. Fund, started by Block Island’s charitable force of nature Mary Donnelly, Block Island nurse for more than 50 years until she retired in 2013 at age 86.

She hasn’t retired from helping year-round Islanders struggling with financial hardship, though. So, as Mary would say, “dress to have a ball,” (we’ve seen everything from tuxedos to Bermuda shorts) and come to The Sullivan House on Saturday, August 7 pm at 11 pm.

You’ll enjoy live entertainment, great food and drinks and a stunning view overlooking the ocean and New Harbor. It’s a great way to honor Mary, support the cause and rub elbows with locals and visitors alike.

Tickets start at $40 and are available at the door, so no need to plan in advance. Just show up and show your stuff on the dance floor— all while showing Mary and her islanders some much-deserved love.

meta.title: Annual Mary D. Ball
meta.description: The Annual Mary D. Ball will be on August 4 at 7 pm.

address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Experience Block Island
summary: This is a sampler platter of all Block Island has to offer -- delicious food, charming lodgings, pristine beaches, fun-filled activities, unique shopping, water sports, biking, mopeds – most of it delivered at a discount, and all of it with a giant dollop of island hospitality.  Lots of special events, too!
content:
This is a sampler platter of all Block Island has to offer -- delicious food, charming lodgings, pristine beaches, fun-filled activities, unique shopping, water sports, biking, mopeds – most of it delivered at a discount, and all of it with a giant dollop of island hospitality.  Lots of special events, too!

The first step is to buy a Taste of Block Island Button, available for just $5 at the Block Island Visitor Center (1 Water Street) or on board either of the ferries that take you out to the island.

This enables you to take advantage of the many discounts offered on this special weekend -- from taxi and farm tours to wine and beer tastings to a chance to design your very own ice cream sundae. Shops will offer discounts, hotels have special lodging packages, and restaurant specials abound.

Outdoor enthusiasts will have an opportunity to learn how to paddleboard, go kayaking or rent bikes and mopeds at a discount.  We could go on for days – which may be why Taste of Block Island is actually a multi-day event, spanning the entire weekend – from Friday, September 21st through Sunday the 23rd.  

This is a popular event, and some of the most popular offerings have limited capacity, so it’s best to reserve early. Check www.blockislandinfo.com to plan your visit and call 401-466-2474 to book any activities that need prior reservations.

meta.title: Fall Taste of Block Island
meta.description: The Fall Taste of Block Island is on September 21 through September 23, 2018.

address: New Shoreham New Shoreham Washington County

author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Lions Club Golf Outing

author: Analyn Alforque
title: Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild Fair
summary: There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here.
content:
There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here. 

If you’d like to take home an artistic reminder of beautiful Block Island, one of the best spots to browse and buy takes place outside of the Block Island Historical Society. Several times each summer, from 9 am to 2 pm, the lawn becomes a showplace and marketplace for members of the Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. Choose among paintings, photographs, sculpture, jewelry, fabric arts, candles, and more. All of the items are handmade by local artists and would make a perfect keepsake for yourself or a gift for the folks back home.

This year, the dates are set for Sundays July 1 and 21, August 18, September 1 and 22 and October 12. There’s no charge for admission to the Fair. For more information about the sale or the treasures inside the Historical Society, call 401.466.2982 or go to www.blockislandhistorical.org.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

content:
There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here. 

If you’d like to take home an artistic reminder of beautiful Block Island, one of the best spots to browse and buy takes place outside of the Block Island Historical Society. Several times each summer, from 9 am to 2 pm, the lawn becomes a showplace and marketplace for members of the Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. Choose among paintings, photographs, sculpture, jewelry, fabric arts, candles, and more. All of the items are handmade by local artists and would make a perfect keepsake for yourself or a gift for the folks back home.

This year, the dates are set for Sundays July 1 and 21, August 18, September 1 and 22 and October 12. There’s no charge for admission to the Fair. For more information about the sale or the treasures inside the Historical Society, call 401.466.2982 or go to www.blockislandhistorical.org.

author: Analyn Alforque
title: Cemetery Tour
summary: "Stories in Stone under the Moonlight"
content:
"Stories in Stone under the Moonlight"

Karin Sprague, Stone Carver and Pam Gasner, Executive Director of the Historical Society lead you on a guided tour through the oldest section of the Island Cemetery.

Please wear comfortable walking shoes and bring a flashlight.  Meet at the north entrance near the skateboard park. 

$15 for Adults, $10 for members and $5 for kids.  There will be door prizes given away.

Taking place rain or shine.

meta.description: Tour the Block Island Cemetary under a full moon.

author: Analyn Alforque
title: Farmer's Markets begin
summary: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.
content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. Try some of the best lemonade on the island - fresh squeezed in front of you.

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. But wait, there's more. Not only are there plenty of edibles to delight your taste buds, there are also arts and crafts and music and kids and dogs and a laid-back community atmosphere that makes you smile just being there. 

Rain or shine, the Market is held every Saturday morning at Legion Park.

meta.title: Farmers Market
meta.description: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

address: Legion Way New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Robert Lucier
title: Hot Dog Eating Contest
summary: As host venue Poor People’s Pub puts it, “Nothing says Happy Birthday ‘merica like competitively eating hot dogs to win bragging rights and a custom Narragansett surfboard.” Okay, so it’s not the prettiest way the Island celebrates America’s independence (that would be the fireworks), but it is one of the most engrossing.
content:
As host venue Poor People’s Pub puts it, “Nothing says Happy Birthday ‘merica like competitively eating hot dogs to win bragging rights and a custom Narragansett surfboard.” Okay, so it’s not the prettiest way the Island celebrates America’s independence (that would be the fireworks), but it is one of the most engrossing. 

Held at 33 Ocean Avenue after the July 4th Parade. 

A word to the wise: this event is not for the faint of heart or sensitive of stomach.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Shad Bloom 10K
summary: This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything: dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall", a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. Questions ? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Kimberly. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.
content:
This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything: dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall", a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. Questions ? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Kimberly. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.

 

 

If the ferry is cancelled race day, then the race will be postponed until Sunday May 15th.
